evals.name: beach-puppy-playable
evals.prompt: make me a lingbot 2 prompt for a golden retriever puppy that you can drive around a sunny beach
evals.expected_output: A playable-character base prompt (camera behind/above, WASD clause, UE5-style anchor) plus actions.json with one action per slot f/g/u/o/space, prompt_en = base + addendum, space = fixed jump string
assertions: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and its 'actions' array covers every slot f, g, u, o, space
every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en, joined verbatim (no paraphrase)
the space action is exactly 'The character jumps high into the air.' with label 'Jump'
the base prompt contains an explicit motion contract tying all motion to key input (WASD / arrow-key clause with 'only while held' or 'in response to keyboard')
the u addendum is a melee strike on a NEARBY object with visible material destruction; the o addendum is a projectile with a trajectory, a DISTANT named target, and impact aftermath
no addendum restates the camera lock or input contract (except the f subject-preservation clause), and each addendum is 1-3 sentences
the base prompt uses the playable-character regime: third-person gameplay video, camera follows from behind and slightly above, and a game-style anchor (e.g. Unreal Engine 5 style)

evals.name: lighthouse-keeper-orbit
evals.prompt: lingbot-world v2 session: an old lighthouse keeper standing at the edge of a cliff at dusk
evals.expected_output: An orbit-regime base prompt (subject locked at centre, arrow-key orbit only while held, freeze clause) plus the five slot actions in the export JSON shape
assertions: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and its 'actions' array covers every slot f, g, u, o, space
every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en, joined verbatim (no paraphrase)
the space action is exactly 'The character jumps high into the air.' with label 'Jump'
the base prompt contains an explicit motion contract tying all motion to key input (WASD / arrow-key clause with 'only while held' or 'in response to keyboard')
the u addendum is a melee strike on a NEARBY object with visible material destruction; the o addendum is a projectile with a trajectory, a DISTANT named target, and impact aftermath
no addendum restates the camera lock or input contract (except the f subject-preservation clause), and each addendum is 1-3 sentences

the base prompt uses the orbit regime: subject locked at the exact centre of the frame at constant size and distance, arrow-key orbit only while held, plus a freeze clause naming specific frozen scene elements

evals.name: moon-astronaut-files
evals.prompt: need a lingbot 2 base prompt + actions.json for an astronaut on the moon, playable, low gravity. save the files to the output dir
evals.expected_output: prompt.txt and actions.json written as files; playable regime; valid JSON matching the export schema
assertions: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and its 'actions' array covers every slot f, g, u, o, space
every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en, joined verbatim (no paraphrase)
the space action is exactly 'The character jumps high into the air.' with label 'Jump'
the base prompt contains an explicit motion contract tying all motion to key input (WASD / arrow-key clause with 'only while held' or 'in response to keyboard')
the u addendum is a melee strike on a NEARBY object with visible material destruction; the o addendum is a projectile with a trajectory, a DISTANT named target, and impact aftermath
no addendum restates the camera lock or input contract (except the f subject-preservation clause), and each addendum is 1-3 sentences
prompt.txt and actions.json are actually written as files in the outputs directory
the base prompt uses the playable-character regime and works low-gravity physics into the movement description

evals.name: ramen-shop-single-shot
evals.prompt: i just need a single lingbot 2 prompt, no action keys or events -- a cozy ramen shop interior at night
evals.expected_output: One standalone base prompt (fixed first-person regime for a place), with the motion contract and freeze clause; NO actions.json and no slot actions
assertions: exactly one prompt is delivered and NO actions.json or slot-action package is produced
the prompt uses the fixed first-person regime appropriate for a place: viewpoint fixed in a single standpoint, arrow-key look-input as the only camera motion, 'only while held'
the prompt contains a freeze clause naming two or three specific scene elements held still (not a generic 'everything is static')
the prompt is one paragraph, present tense, roughly 60-110 words, and populates the scene with concrete secondary objects
the response does not pad the deliverable with unrequested extras (no action slots offered as files, no JSON)
